stories.title: What are Trump's options in Iran as oil stops flowing and allies resist joining war?
stories.description: Iran has effectively shut down the flow of oil from the Gulf region. President Trump hasn't offered a clear solution. Trump is calling for allies to assist the U.S. military in removing the Iranian threat, and many of the allies are resisting.

stories.title: EU rejects Trump's request to help secure the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The European Union has rejected President Trump's request to help secure the Strait of Hormuz, saying the war is "not theirs."

stories.title: Washington Post's David Ignatius says war on Iran won't change the country's regime
stories.description: Michel Martin speaks to Washington Post columnist David Ignatius, who writes that the U.S. war on Iran, despite any tactical success, leaves the regime in place for the foreseeable future.

stories.title: Trump demands NATO and China police the Strait of Hormuz. So far they aren't joining
stories.description: With the Iran war entering a third week, Israel said it plans for at least three more weeks of war, while President Trump demanded other countries help the U.S. secure the vital Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran blocks vital oil route as Israel expands its ground operations in Lebanon
stories.description: As the war in Iran enters its third week, there's no end in sight. Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route, as Trump urges allies to act and Israel expands its ground operations.

stories.title: Iran expert on what the country's leaders think about the war with the US and Israel
stories.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks Iran expert Karim Sadjadpour how leaders in Tehran are thinking about the war with the U.S. and Israel.

stories.title: Iran hits key UAE oil port and Dubai airport
stories.description: The port of Fujairah plays a crucial role in helping keep global supplies moving when the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

stories.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
stories.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

stories.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
stories.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
stories.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

stories.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
stories.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

stories.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
stories.description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defense Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

stories.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
stories.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

stories.title: Bodies of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US torpedo strike to be repatriated
stories.description: The seamen died when Iranian warship the Iris Dena was sunk on 4 March by a US submarine near Sri Lanka.

stories.title: Emirati minister tells BBC Iran must end strikes on Gulf
stories.description: The UAE says more than 1,800 drones and missiles have been projected at the country since the war began.

stories.title: 'Fingers on the trigger': Deadly warnings for Iranians being urged to take action
stories.description: Iran's police chief has warned that his forces would treat anyone taking to the streets "at the enemy's request" as an enemy.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched wide-ranging strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: Oil eases amid signs Iran is allowing some tankers pass through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Global oil prices fell sharply on Monday after a Pakistan-bound oil tanker passed through the Strait of Hormuz with its transponder on. The decline came even as countries have so far rebuffed Donald Trump's request to send warships to escort vessels through the waterway. Meanwhil...

stories.title: China overlooks Trump's demands for Hormuz strait, calls for all sides to de-escalate
stories.description: China on Monday called on all sides to halt military operations in the Strait of Hormuz to avoid further escalation. The remarks came after US President Donald Trump urged China and six other countries to send warships to secure the vital shipping route. Beijing however made no m...

stories.title: Israeli campaign in Lebanon seeks to 'degrade' Hezbollah’s rocket, drone and missile capabilities
stories.description: As Israel expands its military operation against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr. Raz ZIMMT, Director of the Iran and Shiite Axis program at the Institute for National Security Studies (INSS). According to Dr ZIMMT, Israel’s objectives are bas...

stories.title: 'Near universal refusal' - European leaders reject Trump's demand for Strait of Hormuz mission
stories.description: NATO allies and other Western nations pushed back Monday on US President Donald Trump's demand that military alliance members help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the critical conduit for crude oil Iran has effectively closed. FRANCE 24's Dave Keating reports from Brussels

stories.title: Iran's underground arsenal, decentralised military key to press on with strikes on Gulf states
stories.description: Reporting from Doha in Qatar for FRANCE 24, Hoda Abdel-Hamid explains that Iran has been able to keep striking Gulf countries, despite claims made by US President Donald Trump that its arsenal had been decimated, because most of its weapons may be underground and the country has ...

stories.title: Missiles and drone attacks across the UAE as Iran presses on with air campaign in Gulf
stories.description: In the UAE a drone strike sparked a fuel tank fire near Dubai airport, disrupting travel, while a missile killed a civilian in their car in Abu Dhabi, and another drone ignited a fire in an area housing oil infrastructure. Iran is continuing to press its campaign against countrie...

stories.title: Strait talk on Hormuz: Insults, chaos, shadow of Iraq, keep Trump's allies at bay?
stories.description: Day 17 of the war in the Middle East and as the missiles and the drones keep falling, the US President has done a U turn on needing help from his friends and allies. Trump is calling now for a coalition to keep open the Strait of Hormuz. The US strikes on Iran's Kharg Island on F...

stories.title: Trump laments US allies' lack of 'enthusiasm' to help secure Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: US President Donald Trump on Monday criticised US allies on Monday for a lack of enthusiasm in helping unblock the Strait of Hormuz amid the ongoing war against Iran. US and Israeli strikes on Iran have jolted global energy markets, sending oil prices higher and raising gasoline ...

stories.title: US realising it 'needs help from allies even if all-powerful militarily'
stories.description: President Donald Trump criticized US allies on March 16 for their lukewarm response to his call to help protect shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Despite that, he did claim that some countries had agreed to help - but from what has been heard so far, this isn't true as FRANC...

stories.title: Replay: Trump says US dismantled Iran's defense as Middle East war continues
stories.description: Rewatch as US President Donald Trump gives an update on the war against Iran.

stories.title: US allies 'know how to deal with Trump' amidst threats over Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: US President Donald Trump has demanded seven countries send ships to the Strait of Hormuz to secure toe crucial passagepoint for global oil flows. He threatened that refusal to help would mean a 'very bad' future for NATO. But US allies refuse to be dragged into the ongoing war i...

stories.title: ‘Sleeping in our car is the only option’: Displaced Lebanese face skyrocketing rents
stories.description: Hezbollah’s entry into the war against Israel on March 2, in retaliation for the US-Israeli strikes on its Iranian ally, has triggered a massive displacement of civilians across Lebanon. With many shelters at capacity and rental prices skyrocketing, many now find themselves sleep...

stories.title: Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanese town
stories.description: Smoke billowed across southern Lebanon on Monday as the Israeli army said it conducted ground operations in "new locations", deploying troops to target Hezbollah positions as it intensified its campaign against the Iran-backed militant group.

stories.title: ‘War is expensive’: As bombs pummel Lebanon, an ancient souk falls silent
stories.description: Israel's devastating war with Hezbollah has compounded the suffering of ordinary Lebanese who were already grappling with an economy on the brink of collapse. FRANCE 24's reporter travelled to Sidon, a coastal city south of Beirut, to visit the once-bustling alleys of the old sou...

stories.title: 'Kurds can play a significant role in a breakthrough in Iran,' Iranian Kurdish leader says
stories.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, Abdullah Mohtadi, secretary-general of the Iranian Kurdish opposition Komala party, said Kurds "can play a very significant role in making a breakthrough" in Iran. He pointed to "serious signs of weakness" among security forces in Kurdish regions o...

stories.title: Who is answering Trump’s call for help with Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Trump has criticised the response to his call to help protect shipping in the Strait of Hormuz during the war on Iran.

stories.title: How big of a problem is the Strait of Hormuz for Donald Trump?
stories.description: US president frustrated with unenthusiastic response after appealing for help wresting control of key strait from Iran.

stories.title: Hezbollah rocket hits a residential building in Israel’s Nahariya
stories.description: Firefighters battled a blaze at a residential building in Nahariya, Israel after a Hezbollah rocket hit it.

stories.title: Civilians pulled from rubble after US-Israeli strikes on Tehran
stories.description: Rescuers have pulled civilians from the rubble after a new wave of US-Israeli strikes targeted Tehran.

stories.title: US responsible for deadly attack on Iranian school: Amnesty International
stories.description: Rights group says Tomahawk missile likely used in Minab school strike that killed at least 170 people, mostly children.

stories.title: US, Israel now know ‘what kind of nation they are dealing with’
stories.description: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says his country has shown it is ready to take the war “as far as necessary".

stories.title: How badly has the Iran war hit the global economy? The tell-tale signs
stories.description: Oil and gas price hikes due to the war have rattled the global economy.

stories.title: Is Russia benefitting from the Iran war?
stories.description: The US is allowing the purchase of Russian oil that’s stranded at sea after the war on Iran led to higher energy prices.

stories.title: Iran officials tout ‘Trump-burning’ celebration amid battle of narratives
stories.description: Iranian state media calls for turning fire festival into a symbolic protest by burning figures of Trump and Netanyahu.

stories.title: European leaders reject military involvement in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Pushback comes as US President Donald Trump says NATO allies should help secure key waterway amid soaring oil prices.

stories.title: US says it has destroyed Iran missile capacity: How is Iran still shooting?
stories.description: Despite degraded launch capabilities, Iran has enough missiles to fire strategically, keep region on edge, say experts.

stories.title: Qatar calls on Iran to stop Gulf attacks, urges diplomatic solution
stories.description: Qatar foreign ministry spokesman Majed al-Ansari accuses Iran of targeting civilian areas as he calls for de-escalation.

stories.title: The US-Israeli strategy against Iran is working. Here is why
stories.description: Every aspect of Iran’s ability to project regional power is being successfully degraded.

stories.title: Qatar insists Iran is targeting civilian areas
stories.description: Qatar’s Foreign Ministry spokesperson Majed Al Ansari says Iranian attacks have targeted residential areas.

stories.title: Trump upset as key US partners shun call for Hormuz warship escorts
stories.description: Several US allies rebuffed Donald Trump’s call on Monday to send warships to escort shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, drawing criticism from the US president, who accused Western partners of ingratitude after decades of support. The US-Israeli war on Iran is in its third wee...

stories.title: Hamas holds talks with Trump-led board as Iran war strains Gaza plan: sources
stories.description: The envoys from US President Donald Trump’s “Board of Peace” have met representatives of Hamas in Cairo in an effort to safeguard the Gaza ceasefire, under serious strain since the United States and Israel began bombing Iran, three sources told Reuters. The weekend meeting is the...

stories.title: As India seeks Hormuz safe passage, Tehran asks for return of seized tankers, sources say
stories.description: Iran has asked India to release three tankers seized in February as part of talks ​seeking the safe passage of Indian‑flagged or India‑bound vessels out of the Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz, three sources ‌with knowledge of the matter told Reuters. Indian authorities seized the t...

stories.title: Tehran thanks Islamabad for extending ‘strong solidarity’ with Iranian govt, people facing US-Israeli aggression
stories.description: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Monday thanked the government and people of Pakistan for extending their “strong solidarity and support” with Iran and its people who he said were facing aggression from the United States and Israel. In a post in Urdu on X, Araghchi said:...

stories.title: Trump says talks with Cuba ongoing, action possible after Iran
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said on Sunday that the United States could soon reach a deal with Cuba or take other action, signaling that developments in the long-strained relationship may come quickly. “Cuba also wants to make a deal, and I think we will pretty soon either make a d...

stories.title: Press review: Europe seeks to block US-Russia thaw as Russia eyes role in US-Iran talks
stories.description: Top stories from the Russian press on Friday, June 6th

stories.title: Israel to continue striking Beirut until Lebanon disarms Hezbollah — defense minister
stories.description: "Agreements must be honored and the Lebanese government does not do what is required, Israel will continue to act, and with great force," Israel Katz said

stories.title: Orban sees EU bracing for another decade of countering Russia amid proxy war in Ukraine
stories.description: Commenting on the Ukraine crisis, the Hungarian PM said that Brussels viewed Ukraine as "the first line of European defense" where pan-European interests were currently being defended

stories.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah targets in Beirut suburbs — IDF
stories.description: Prior to that, the Israeli army issued a warning for this neighborhood, telling residents to leave their homes immediately

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
